title: Catalyst Center + ThousandEyes Network Path Correlation
dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:networkhealth`. from the `networkhealth` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/network-health`. Default poll interval: 900s (15m). Volume: ~1 event per poll (aggregate network score). RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `healthScore` (integer 0-100), `goodCount` (integer), `badCount` (integer), `totalCount` (integer). Additionally correlates with ThousandEyes test data via `index=thousandeyes sourcetype=thousandeyes:*` (Cisco ThousandEyes TA). Requires both Catalyst Center and ThousandEyes data collection.
app: `Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7538) + `Cisco ThousandEyes App for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7719)
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:networkhealth" | stats latest(healthScore) as internal_health by _time | appendcols [search `stream_index` thousandeyes.test.type="agent-to-server" | stats avg(network.latency) as avg_latency_s avg(network.loss) as avg_loss by _time | eval avg_latency_ms=round(avg_latency_s*1000,1) | eval loss_pct=round(avg_loss*100,1)] | where internal_health < 70 AND (avg_latency_ms > 100 OR loss_pct > 5) | eval isolation=if(internal_health<70 AND avg_latency_ms<50, "Internal network issue", if(internal_health>=70 AND avg_latency_ms>100, "External path issue", "Both internal and external issues")) | table _time internal_health avg_latency_ms loss_pct isolation
description: Correlates Catalyst Center internal network health with ThousandEyes external path quality to isolate whether performance problems are inside the campus network or on the WAN/internet path.
value: The hardest troubleshooting question is 'is it us or them?' Correlating internal health (Catalyst Center) with external path quality (ThousandEyes) answers this question in seconds.
implementation:
1. **Catalyst Center (TA 7538):** `cisco:dnac:networkhealth` on `index=catalyst` with `healthScore` (UC-5.13.16).
2. **ThousandEyes (app 7719):** Install and connect Te OTel/HTTP to Splunk per app docs; create or confirm the **`stream_index`** macro to point at the index containing Te agent-to-server test metrics (often a dedicated HEC/OTel index).
3. **Field model:** The SPL uses `thousandeyes.test.type` and `network.latency` / `network.loss` in OTel-style JSON — if your app normalizes to different field names, update the `stats` to match (e.g. `avg(latency_ms)`).
4. **Thresholds:** Tune `70`, `100` ms, and `5%` loss to your SLAs and baseline noise.
visualization: Table of internal_health, avg_latency_ms, loss_pct, isolation; optional Sankey or treemap of isolation reason counts; timechart overlay: internal_health vs Te latency.

references.title: Catalyst Center Network Health API — Cisco DevNet

knownFalsePositives:
**ThousandEyes test agent offline or unreachable causing missing network path data.** If a ThousandEyes cloud or enterprise agent is unavailable, the network path correlation will show gaps. Distinguish by checking `index=thousandeyes` for agent-health events or missing test results. Do not suppress — an offline ThousandEyes agent is its own operational issue.

**ThousandEyes measuring internet/cloud path quality while Catalyst Center measures campus LAN health.** A degradation in ThousandEyes scores (internet latency, cloud service reachability) does not necessarily correlate with Catalyst Center campus health. The divergence may be correct: the campus is healthy but the internet path is degraded. Distinguish by checking whether the ThousandEyes tests target internet endpoints or internal campus targets. No suppression needed — the divergence is informative.

**Different time granularity between ThousandEyes and Catalyst Center data.** ThousandEyes tests may run at 1-5 minute intervals while Catalyst Center polls at 15 minutes. The `appendcols` join may produce misaligned time comparisons. Distinguish by comparing the latest event timestamps from both sources. Suppress by binning both data sources to a common time span (e.g., 15 minutes) before correlation.

**ThousandEyes alert-only data appearing without baseline context.** If the ThousandEyes integration sends only alert/degradation events (not continuous health data), the correlation will only trigger during problems, not during normal operation. Distinguish by checking whether ThousandEyes data is continuous or event-driven. Suppress by noting the asymmetric data model in the dashboard description.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) with `devicehealth` or `interfacehealth` inputs enabled to `index=catalyst`.
• Cisco ThousandEyes App for Splunk (Splunkbase 6498) with test results data in `index=thousandeyes` (or equivalent), sourcetype `cisco:thousandeyes:*`.
• Both API credentials configured — Catalyst Center service account and ThousandEyes OAuth 2.0 bearer token.
• ThousandEyes agents deployed on or near the network paths managed by Catalyst Center for meaningful correlation.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
This UC correlates infrastructure health (Catalyst Center) with path-level performance (ThousandEyes):
• **Catalyst Center side:** `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:devicehealth` — polled at 900s intervals.
• **ThousandEyes side:** `sourcetype=cisco:thousandeyes:test:results` — polled or streamed via the ThousandEyes TA.

Key fields for correlation:
• Catalyst Center: `deviceName`, `managementIpAddress`, `overallHealth`.
• ThousandEyes: `testName`, `agentName`, `serverIp`, `avgLatency`, `loss`, `jitter`, `errorType`.
• Correlation: `managementIpAddress` (Catalyst Center) ↔ `serverIp` (ThousandEyes target) or agent-to-site mapping via lookup.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:devicehealth"
| stats latest(overallHealth) as cc_health latest(reachabilityHealth) as cc_reach by deviceName, managementIpAddress, siteId
| join managementIpAddress type=left [search index=thousandeyes sourcetype="cisco:thousandeyes:*" | stats avg(avgLatency) as te_latency avg(loss) as te_loss latest(errorType) as te_error by serverIp | rename serverIp as managementIpAddress]
| eval path_status=case(isnotnull(te_error) AND te_error!="","Path Error", te_loss>5,"High Loss", te_latency>200,"High Latency", cc_health<50,"Infra Degraded", 1==1,"Healthy")
| where path_status!="Healthy"
| sort path_status, cc_health
```

Understanding this SPL:
• **`join managementIpAddress type=left`**: Correlates Catalyst Center device health with ThousandEyes path metrics targeting the same IP. Left join ensures Catalyst Center devices without ThousandEyes tests still appear.
• **`te_error`**: ThousandEyes error types (e.g., "DNS resolution error", "Connect error") indicate path failures that may correlate with Catalyst Center health degradation.
• **`where path_status!="Healthy"`**: Filters to only show devices/paths with issues, making the output actionable.

Step 3 — Validate
• **Cross-reference:** Select 3-5 devices monitored by both Catalyst Center and ThousandEyes. Compare the health and path metrics in Splunk with the respective vendor UIs.
• **Join completeness:** If few devices match, the `managementIpAddress` may not correspond to the ThousandEyes test target. Consider a `catalyst_te_agent_map` lookup.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Dashboard:** Combined health view showing infrastructure health alongside path performance. Add ThousandEyes path visualization (latency heatmap) next to Catalyst Center device health tiles.
• **Root cause analysis:** When both Catalyst Center and ThousandEyes show degradation for the same device/path, the root cause is likely in the infrastructure. When only ThousandEyes shows issues, the problem may be upstream (ISP, cloud provider).

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• **No ThousandEyes data in the join:** Verify the ThousandEyes TA is collecting data: `index=thousandeyes earliest=-1h | stats count`. Check that `serverIp` fields match `managementIpAddress` values.
• **ThousandEyes data stale:** The ThousandEyes TA may use different poll intervals than the Catalyst Center TA. Ensure both cover the same time window.
• **False path errors:** ThousandEyes may report errors for scheduled test maintenance. Cross-reference with ThousandEyes alert suppression windows.
grandmaExplanation: We combine your internal network health with external internet path quality to quickly determine whether a problem is inside your network or on the way to the cloud.
